summary: Verified RBAC unification (iteration 318). Backend GET /api/roles returns 107 module_labels and 19 categories including all 6 new categories (Fleet, Integrations, Communication, Presentation, Accounting, Sales Operations Extras). All 24 of the 25 newly-added keys from the review are correctly registered and backfilled into every existing role. The 25th key `platform_admin` is registered in MODULE_LABELS and MODULE_CATEGORIES but is MISSING from DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS in backend/models/role.py, so the backfill loop never writes it into any role's permissions dict — the Role Management UI renders a toggle for it but the state is undefined/false for every role including Admin. Frontend: as CEO surya.yadavalli, sidebar shows all 7 explicitly-named Admin-module items (Vehicles, Drivers, Batch Genealogy, Vendor Types, Reversals Log, Notification Templates, Sharing Recipients); all 12 requested routes load successfully (no 'Module Not Available' or 'Access Denied' banners); Role Management UI at /tenant-settings → Roles surfaces all 6 new categories with editable toggles.

minor.issue: 'platform_admin' is listed in MODULE_CATEGORIES['Admin'] and MODULE_LABELS but NOT in DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS, so backfill (routes/roles.py L55-67) never adds it to any role's permissions. Every role (Admin, Manager, User, Viewer, Creative Director) is missing this key in permissions{}. Role Management UI will show a toggle whose state can't be read via hasRolePermission('platform_admin'). Note: the /platform-admin route itself is gated by an email allowlist (PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS), not by moduleKey, so this doesn't block the platform admin page — but the toggle in Role Management is a no-op ghost.

action_items: Add 'platform_admin' to DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS in /app/backend/models/role.py (e.g., {view: False, create: False, edit: False, delete: False}) so backfill wires it into all roles. Alternatively, if platform_admin is intentionally excluded from tenant-role gating (already handled via PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS), remove it from MODULE_CATEGORIES and MODULE_LABELS to avoid a ghost toggle in Role Management.

Optional cleanup: pre-existing custom role 'Creative Director' in the Nyla tenant is missing the 'is_system_role' field in its DB doc. Not caused by this iteration but the Role model default (False) is not persisted. Consider a one-time migration to set is_system_role explicitly on all documents so the API contract stays consistent.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/routes/roles.py backfill loop only iterates DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS.items() — any key that appears only in MODULE_LABELS/MODULE_CATEGORIES (like the current platform_admin) will silently miss backfill. Consider driving the backfill from the union of DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS keys AND every key referenced in MODULE_CATEGORIES to catch this class of drift automatically.

backend/models/role.py L302-320 (MODULE_CATEGORIES) and L323-431 (MODULE_LABELS) live separately from DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS. Add an assertion or module-load sanity check: assert set(MODULE_LABELS.keys()) == set(DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS.keys()) == {k for v in MODULE_CATEGORIES.values() for k in v}. This would have caught the platform_admin drift at import time.

frontend App.js ProtectedRoute (L216) uses hasRolePermission(moduleKey) with default false when the key is absent, so a missing backfill silently blocks access. If backend guarantees backfill this is fine, but a defensive fallback (e.g., grant when key absent and role is Admin/CEO) would avoid surprises during future rollout.

success_rate.backend: 11/15 pytest cases pass (73%). 3 failures traced to the single platform_admin backfill gap; 1 failure ('Creative Director' missing is_system_role) is pre-existing DB data, unrelated to this iteration.
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 12 requested routes render, sidebar populated with new items in Admin module, Role Management UI surfaces all 6 new categories.

seed_data_creation: None. No writes to production data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once main agent adds platform_admin to DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS (or removes it from labels/categories), rerun /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_318_rbac_unification.py — the 3 platform_admin failures should clear. The 1 remaining failure ('Creative Director missing is_system_role') is orthogonal DB drift. Modules discovered via aside module-switcher: Sales, Production, Distribution, Marketing, Accounting, Admin. Admin module sidebar groups: Fleet (Vehicles, Drivers), Product & SKU (SKU Management, Replace SKU, Batch Genealogy), Master Data (Locations, Lead Statuses, Design Request Types, Print Settings, Business Categories, Vendor Types, Contact Categories, Expense Categories, COGS Components), plus Reversals Log, Notification Templates, Sharing Recipients, Tenant Settings, Zoho/Slack/Google Drive/State Machines.
rca_of_the_issue: backend/routes/roles.py backfill loop (lines 55-67) writes only keys present in DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS into each role's permissions dict. The main agent added 'platform_admin' to MODULE_CATEGORIES (line 310) and MODULE_LABELS (line 430) but forgot to add it to DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS. Reproduction: GET /api/roles as any user; inspect roles[*].permissions — none contain the key 'platform_admin'. Mitigation: add "platform_admin": {view: False, create: False, edit: False, delete: False} to DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS.
